tools.name: future_value
tools.description: Calculate future value of an investment with compound interest. Use this for investment growth projections, savings account growth, and retirement planning. For comparing multiple rates and periods, use future_value_table instead.

rate.description: Annual interest rate as decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%)

years.description: Number of years for the investment

tools.name: present_value
tools.description: Calculate present value (current worth) of a future amount. Use this for loan valuation, bond pricing, and investment analysis. For comparing multiple scenarios, use future_value_table with different periods and rates instead.

rate.description: Annual discount rate as decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%)

years.description: Number of years until the amount is received

tools.name: discount
tools.description: Calculate final price after applying a percentage discount. Use this for retail pricing, sale calculations, and cost savings analysis. For comparing multiple discount percentages, use discount_table instead.

discount.description: Discount percentage (e.g., 20 for 20% or 0.20 as decimal, depending on input format)

tools.name: markup
tools.description: Calculate selling price from cost and markup percentage. Use this for product pricing, profit margin calculation, and business pricing strategy. For comparing multiple markup scenarios, use discount_table which also shows profit margins.

markup.description: Markup percentage (e.g., 30 for 30% or 0.30 as decimal, depending on input format)

tools.name: future_value_table
tools.description: Generate a comparison table of future values across multiple interest rates and time periods. Use this for investment scenario comparison, rate shopping, and long-term planning. For single calculations, use future_value instead.

periods.description: Time periods in years to compare (e.g., [1, 5, 10, 20])

tools.name: discount_table
tools.description: Compare multiple discount percentages for the same price. Use this for bulk pricing strategies, promotional planning, and price comparison. For single discount calculations, use discount instead.

tools.name: launch_ui
tools.description: Launch the interactive web UI for the financial calculator. Use this for visual, tab-based calculations with real-time results and beautiful gradient UI. For command-line calculations, use the individual calculation tools instead.

port.description: Port number for the web server (default: 5050)

messages.content: Hi, I'm putting together a pricing proposal for a client and need some help figuring out the right selling price. It's for a complete workspace design service package - I have my costs outlined, but I want to use markup pricing to set my final price. Can you help me work through that?

I'd be happy to help you work out the selling price using markup pricing! To calculate that, I'll need two pieces of information:

1. **Your cost** - What does it cost you to deliver the workspace design service package? (This could include labor, materials, software, any subcontractors, etc.)

2. **Your markup percentage** - What percentage markup would you like to apply to reach your target profit margin?

Once you provide those, I can calculate the selling price for you right away!

Now I need to help a client with office furniture purchases. They're looking at different discount options and want to compare them to get the best value. I have a few quotes from suppliers - one offers 15% off the list price, another has a buy-3-get-1-free deal, and there's a third option with a flat $200 discount per item when buying at least 5. Can you help me figure out which option would actually cost the least for ordering, say, 6 ergonomic chairs?

Great question! To compare all three options properly, I'll need one key piece of information:

**What is the list price per chair?**

Once you provide that, I can calculate the total cost under each option and determine which gives the best value for 6 chairs.

In the meantime, here's how each option would work:

| Option | How it works |
|--------|--------------|
| **15% off** | Simple percentage discount off the list price |
| **Buy-3-get-1-free** | For every 4 chairs, you pay for 3. With 6 chairs: you'd get 1 free (paying for 5 total) |
| **$200 off (min. 5)** | If buying 6, each chair gets $200 off the list price |

Just let me know the list price per chair and I'll run the numbers to show you exactly which option saves the most!
The list price for each ergonomic chair is $350.

Now I can compare all three options for 6 chairs at $350 each:

| Option | Calculation | Total Cost |
|--------|-------------|------------|
| **15% off** | 6 × $350 = $2,100 → minus 15% ($315) | **$1,785** |
| **Buy-3-get-1-free** | 6 chairs = Pay for 5 (1 free in a set of 4) → 5 × $350 | **$1,750** |
| **$200 off (min 5)** | 6 × ($350 - $200) = 6 × $150 | **$900** |

---

**Winner: Option 3** — The $200-per-item discount wins by a huge margin, saving your client **$850** compared to the next best option!

A few things to confirm with the third supplier:
- Is the $200 discount available on *every* item when buying 5+, or is it a one-time flat discount?
- Are there any minimum quantity requirements or other terms?

If the $200 is truly per item, it's an easy decision. Want me to run any other scenarios (like different quantities)?

That $200 discount is per item - so each chair would get the $200 off when buying 5 or more. And there are no other hidden fees or minimums that I know of on that quote. This definitely seems like the best option for my client. Thanks for running those numbers - I can confidently recommend the third supplier to them now.

[TASK_END]

That's great news! Your client will save significantly with the third supplier — $900 total for 6 chairs is a fantastic deal compared to the other options.

Best of luck with the pricing proposal! If you need help with any other calculations — whether it's markup pricing for your services, comparing more discount scenarios, or anything else — feel free to reach out.

Happy to help! 🎉
